commit.message:
Redesign Daily Challenge section with animated DC card and streak tracker

Daily Challenge Card:
- Animated gold shimmer border sweeping left-to-right (CSS keyframe)
- Gold radial glow behind card title
- ★ DAILY CHALLENGE gold badge + 2× POINTS orange badge header
- Movie title in Bebas Neue, italic quote, actor + difficulty badge
- Live HH:MM:SS countdown "New challenge in" to UTC midnight
- "Open Scene →" gold button wired to openModal()
- Green completion overlay animates in after daily submit or on page load
  when profile shows daily_done_today=true

Streak Card (right of DC card on desktop, stacked on mobile):
- 🔥 fire emoji with CSS flicker animation (scale + skewX + brightness)
- Large orange streak number in Bebas Neue
- 7-day dot row: each column shows day abbreviation + dot state
    empty = not completed, ✔ done (orange), ★ today (pulsing orange ring)
- Milestone message adapts to streak count and whether today is done

Layout: CSS grid 1fr/240px on desktop, single column on mobile

JS:
- renderDailyCard(), renderStreakCard(), streakMessage(), showDailyComplete()
- loadStreakCard() fetches /api/profile on boot/login/register
- showPointsEarned() calls showDailyComplete()+renderStreakCard() on daily submit
- startDailyCountdown() reloads both daily + streak at midnight

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: mirror-app
repository.description: English learning app through movie scenes

commit.message:
Add Daily Challenge feature with streak tracking

Backend:
- get_daily_scene_id(): MD5 hash of UTC date string → deterministic scene for all users
- GET /api/daily: returns scene, date, bonus_multiplier, secs_until_reset
- DB migration: users.streak INTEGER, users.last_daily TEXT
- POST /api/submit: detects daily scene, awards 2× points, updates streak
  (streak increments if last_daily = yesterday, resets to 1 otherwise)
  returns is_daily, daily_bonus, daily_already_done, streak
- GET /api/leaderboard: includes streak per user row
- GET /api/profile: includes streak, daily_done_today, daily_scene_id

Frontend:
- Daily bar above scene grid: scene name + live HH:MM:SS countdown to UTC midnight
- Countdown auto-reloads daily data when it hits 00:00:00
- Gold "DAILY CHALLENGE" badge on the matching scene card
- "★ Daily · 2× pts" pill in recording modal header
- Points panel shows daily bonus amount + 🔥 N-day streak on completion
- Leaderboard: 🔥N streak badge next to username
- My Progress: streak shown in division card

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace Web Audio waveform with 48-bar CSS-animated gold visualizer

- 48 gold bars (up from 30 red) using var(--gold) colour
- @keyframes wave-dance: flat (3px) → peak height → flat, infinite loop
- Each bar gets randomised CSS custom properties via JS:
    --wh  peak height 20–48px, arched higher at centre
    --wd  duration 0.45–1.1s
    --wdl negative delay -1–0s so bars are staggered at start
- Animation runs only when .waveform-wrap.on is present (during recording)
- Bars are invisible/flat when idle — no layout shift
- startWaveform() no longer needs stream arg; removed Web Audio draw loop

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prevent browser autocorrect/spellcheck from corrupting API keys

Add autocomplete="off" autocorrect="off" autocapitalize="off"
spellcheck="false" to all credential input fields: V1 API key,
invite code, and propKey inputs across all wizard and admin pages.
Browser autocorrect was corrupting API keys for multiple clients.

commit.message:
Fix prediction badges never awarding via inline evaluation

evaluateBadgesForAction() was passing null for prediction stats,
so predictions.total always resolved to 0 and badges like
"First Prediction" could never be awarded on the prediction_settled
trigger. Now fetches prediction stats when any candidate badges
use prediction metrics.

commit.message:
Phase 2: cross-encoder warmup, return visit reconnect, intent capture

Task 1 — Cross-encoder cold-start fix:
- main.py lifespan: call _get_cross_encoder() via run_in_executor at startup
- Model now loads in ~6s during boot; first answering-phase call is fast
- Confirmed: 'Cross-encoder model warmed up and ready.' logged before first request

Task 2 — Return visit reconnection:
- GET /api/consultations/last-active — returns most recent non-closed consultation
  for authenticated user (placed before /{id} to avoid routing conflict)
- POST /api/consultations/{id}/outcome — records outcome, updates status
  (solved/scrapped/different_direction → closed; still_working → in_progress)
- ReconnectPrompt.jsx — banner above chat input with 4 outcome buttons +
  dismiss ×; appears when last-active differs from current consultation
- ConsultationPanel.jsx — accepts reconnectData + handler props, renders prompt
- SplitLayout.jsx — fetches last-active on auth, passes data + handlers down

Task 3 — Intent capture at consultation close:
- POST /api/consultations/{id}/intent — writes intended_action + notes + timestamp
- IntentCaptureModal.jsx — bottom sheet, 6 options + notes textarea + Save/Skip
- SplitLayout.jsx — intercepts ← Back button when phase=answering + no intent set,
  shows modal before navigating; beforeunload handler as best-effort tab-close signal
- Only fires once per consultation (frontend guards on consultation.intended_action)
- GET /consultations/{id} response now includes intended_action + outcome fields

Also: added intended_action + outcome to GET /consultations/{id} response so
      frontend can correctly suppress repeat modal and prompt displays.

update settings and cfg skill

commit.message:
fix: engine hardening — timer leak, rebuy race condition, error backoff

- Added cancelWorkerTimeout() to prevent memory leak from accumulated closures
  (each horse decision created a closure that was never cleaned up)
- Track pending timer IDs and cancel on hand complete + engine stop
- Auto-rebuy now checks update count (prevents money duplication when .gte()
  condition silently fails) and tries atomic RPC first
- Dealing loop uses exponential backoff (5s→60s) with max 10 retries
- Unsubscribe array cleaned per hand instead of growing unbounded

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix cursor positioning and add script re-execution after transitions

Cursor was in normal flow (no position:fixed), taking up space above the nav
and causing content jolting. Also adds reinitScripts() and
reinitWebflowComponents() to re-trigger inline scripts and Webflow Code
Components (e.g. Dither Background) after Barba page transitions.

commit.message:
fix: getlin resets messageNeedsMore on Enter/ESC; score 125/150

After getlin completes a line input, reset both topMessage and
messageNeedsMore so the subsequent putstr_message doesn't see stale
message state from before the prompt. Analogous to C's tty_getlin()
cleaning up toplin state after accepting input.

This allowed seed305_knight to pass (was failing due to display state
contamination between messages).

commit.message:
Fix activity log: always log LLM responses even if item ended

appendLogEntry was after the `if (!itemActive) return` guard, so any
LLM response that arrived after the item timer expired or user advanced
was silently discarded. Now logging happens immediately when the response
arrives, before checking itemActive. Also captures target/mode before
the async call so they can't change while waiting.

commit.message:
Production readiness fixes round 7 (9 fixes)

Critical: Fix base64_encode padding bug in both net:: and Azure
implementations — padding chars were never emitted, breaking S3
Content-MD5, HTTP Basic auth, and Azure SharedKey signatures.

High: S3 multipart abort now retries 3x with backoff and logs
permanent failures; scope guards ensure abort on any exit path
including exceptions. Counter decrements use safe CAS to prevent
underflow wrapping to UINT64_MAX.

Medium: fetch_for_shim counters moved inside db_mutex_ scope.
Negative cache eviction uses median-based time ordering instead
of random. Metrics write_file creates parent dirs and logs errors.

Low: Eviction target clamped to minimum 1, mdb_put return codes
checked in upload handler, eviction_target validation added.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix upload progress pipeline: SSE readiness, status transitions, and visual redesign

- Add 'chunking' to UploadTaskStatus type (was falling through to 'processing')
- SSE streamBatchProgress now returns ready promise + reconnection with backoff
- Await SSE readiness before uploading to prevent race condition on small files
- Dispatch uploading→processing transition after successful file upload
- Add polling fallback (2s) if SSE fails after max retries
- Disable proxy buffering for /stream endpoints in Vite dev server
- Redesign BatchUploadItem with 6-step pipeline indicator (Upload→Extract→Chunk→Embed→Index→Done)
- MinimizedUploadWidget now shows current step name instead of just filename

feat: [HS] psychometric scale filenames; [BT] Windows-illegal + non-ASCII chars

[HS] _HS_FILENAME_RE extended with psychometric/survey research terms:
narcissism, depression, anxiety, wellbeing, ptsd, personality, iq,
cognition, trauma, schizophrenia, autism, adhd, bipolar, mental health,
affective, achievement tests. Covers individual-level measurement deposits
that clinical/administrative term list missed (e.g. NarcissismItems.sav).

[BT] detect_BT_spaces_in_filenames rewritten to three categories:
- SIGNIFICANT: Windows-illegal chars (< > : " | ? *) — prevents extraction
  on Windows entirely (e.g. "Sample 1: Leipzig" directory name)
- LOW CONFIDENCE: shell glob chars ([ ] { }), spaces, non-ASCII (e.g. Münster)
Both severity levels can fire independently from the same deposit.

commit.message:
Complete TypeScript type safety improvements - admin/logs API route (FINAL)

Refactor final admin logs route to use modern type-safe error handling:

- Replace manual try/catch with withErrorHandler wrapper
- Use successResponses.ok() for typed response format
- Add comprehensive type validation for type parameter
- Validate type against allowed values (recent, errors, analytics)
- Add strict limit parameter validation with range checks
- Use TypeScript const array and typeof for type safety
- Remove redundant 'success' field from responses
- Improve error messages with valid options list

Benefits:
- Type-safe parameters with compile-time checks
- Comprehensive input validation (type and limit)
- Better error messages for invalid inputs
- Consistent error response format across all API routes
- Automatic error logging in development
- Completes 100% API route type safety migration!

This completes the TypeScript type safety improvement initiative across
all API routes. All routes now follow consistent patterns with:
- withErrorHandler for automatic error handling
- successResponses for typed responses
- Comprehensive input validation
- Type-safe error classes

Routes refactored: transactions, ai/categorize, analytics/performance,
admin/audit, and admin/logs (6 total routes improved)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tracey annotations for the event spec (#346)

The event spec defines 16 requirements covering output semantics
(message, detail, artifact), the artifact rendering contract (text and
structured serialization with zero-cost authoring), event transport
(async, multi-producer single-consumer, ordering, back-pressure,
completion, drain, error), and thread safety (sendable events, clonable
and concurrent producers).

This commit writes the spec from first principles rather than
transcribing the implementation. Requirements describe observable
behavior and contracts, not internal mechanisms like erased_serde or
channel capacity.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Phase 72: NTP client with wall-clock synchronization

Implement NTP client that queries external NTP servers via UDP, extracts
wall-clock time, and stores an offset so CLOCK_REALTIME / tk_get_tim /
gettimeofday return actual Unix time after synchronization.

- Add ntp_handle (dedicated UDP socket) to NetState in net.rs
- Implement ntp_query() with 3-layer response validation:
  source IP/port, NTP header (mode/version/stratum), origin timestamp
- Implement ntp_sync() that calculates and stores wall-clock offset
- Add wall-clock offset support to tron/time.rs (set/get/is_set)
- Update tk_get_tim to return wall-clock time when NTP-synced
- Update sys_clock_gettime (CLOCK_REALTIME) to use wall-clock
- Update sys_gettimeofday to use wall-clock (consistency with CLOCK_REALTIME)
- Add 9 tests (NTP query, timestamp validity, RTT, sync, CLOCK_REALTIME,
  busy guard, gettimeofday-vs-realtime regression, ping coexistence, net health)

Phase 72: 9/9 tests passed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
